reviews.review_description: I absolutely love ping pong/table tennis IRL and getting into a heated back and forth rally is the heart and soul of the sport, IMO, and the rallies in this game are a lot of fun! This is a welcome addition to the list of enjoyable and fun table tennis games! So far, the ONLY issue I am seeing is the position of my paddle. It is just awkward, and no matter which way I adjust it in settings, it still doesn’t feel right. That being said, I have been having a blast playing, but the game will be that much better if an update comes to allow complete customization of the paddle position. When that happens, I will immediately bring my 4 stars up to 5 stars! Nice effort, devs!

reviews.review_title: Realistic Table Tennis Training in VR!
reviews.review_description:
What sets Timo Boll VR Table Tennis apart is its attention to realism — especially in the avatars. No more disembodied hands and floating heads — you finally get to face a lifelike human opponent across the table, which really boosts immersion.

But the true star of the game is its training module. You’re not just swinging randomly — you’re learning real table tennis technique. With Timo Boll himself demonstrating each move, you can practice, refine, and eventually master shots through various difficulty levels. The game even introduces you to the real lingo of the sport, helping you correct your stance and improve your technical skill like a pro.

Multiplayer mode is now in beta, and the studio is actively seeking feedback to fine-tune the experience. It’s rare to find a VR sports game with this much depth and ongoing support — well done to the dev team! 🏓

￼DEVS! If you want people to buy and support this game, you’ve gotta make an option to have more realistic physics. Theyre basically unusable as of now. There is nothing inaccessible about ping-pong with normal physics, I mean look at Eleven TT, it’s got one of the largest VR user bases ever- and with Timo Boll attached to this game the most exciting part was that you might be able to learn how to hit the ball correctly and actually “play with him “. It’s sort of counterintuitive that you would make the physics unrealistic. ￼People have always played ping-pong at all levels from very beginner to pro with “IRL physics” and this has never been a problem.￼ I’m a huge table tennis fan in real life and enjoy Eleven table tennis and have a solid slime adapter, and play 2 to 3 times a week IRL. When I saw that Timo Boll had a game with him in it … that you could train with him(!!) I immediately pre-ordered the game. But after waiting three excited months past when the game was supposed to be delivered (they delayed it for quite a few months )￼ I was so amazingly disappointed to find the lack of realism. This game has so so much potential as a separate thing from Eleven TT: A chance to practice your IRL strokes, to do a story / competition PVE mode, and more. Making the physics some sort of weird unrealistic floaty thing makes it a totally different game from TT entirely. I am sincerely hoping I will come back to this game in a month or two and the physics will be more realistic- and that game would get 5* in seconds from me. But for now this will gather dust while I play Eleven. Please consider this devs! Please for yours and my sake! :)

developer_response.body: Hello, Thanks for your feedback. We're constantly improving the physics. Feel free to share some more details on what you don't like about the physics. Either mention here or send us an email info@timo.games. All the best, Timo Boll Interactive Games.

reviews.review_description: I was hoping for something like Mike Tyson punchout or how Ronaldo was featured in the Fatal Furys. But this one is still too obsessed by being realistic. A very solid game though. At least it's quite different from the usually suspects.

I really like the opponent simulation and also the training with Timo. I've only been playing for a short time, but it shows great promise.
Please add more adapters: Pongori 500 Q2. Also, please create a tutorial and improve how to change paddle parameters to properly adapt the controllers, otherwise the game won't play well.
Thank you very much.

developer_response.body: Hello, Thank you for positive feedback. We're happy you like the experience. We are constantly updating the app and we're trying to support as many adapters as possible. All the best, Timo Boll Interactive Games

reviews.review_description: Timo, how… how could you let this have your name on it. Arcade or not arcade this is terrible. You just needed to get the devs behind “tenbillion” and improve on their physics a bit. But this… :(

developer_response.body: Hello, Thanks for your feedback. Could please give us some more details on what exactly you don't like about the physics. We're working hard to satisfy all our customers. Feel free to share more details here or drop us an email at info@timo.games. Your feedback is much appreciated. All the best, Timo Boll Interactive Games

reviews.review_description:
I really like Timo Boll's avatar and his voice. I enjoy the campaign mode that allows you to progress through levels and test your skills. I also appreciate the fully animated avatars of the opponents, whose realistic movements make it feel like you're playing against real players.

At first, I couldn't hit the ball at all—the controller grip and paddle position didn't match up. But after tweaking the settings until they felt right, it became a really fun and realistic ping pong game. The game offers both VR and MR modes.

Currently, it doesn't support multiplayer and still has a few things that need improvement. However, if the developers continue to update it, this could become a top-tier option for anyone wanting to play ping pong in VR.

if I compare this to eleven table tennis, elevens physics beats this hands down. the physics here feels  floaty and you can not hit hard or it goes flying. 

why does the game seem to assume you have a paddle adapter? in order to play without one, you have to click on unlock on the left side of the screen and adjust the settings. . multiplayer is in beta, it needs a lot of work. it doesn't seem to be tracking the players paddle good enough and half the time the ball will come back to you out of the air because it doesn't look like it even hits the opponents paddle. 

I only have maybe an hour in the game so far but it does not feel like real table tennis to me and that is sad. I had high hopes for this game, let's hope they are able to keep improving it.

developer_response.body:
Hi! 

Thanks for your feedback. We're sorry that the experience doesn’t meet your expectations. We're already working on the adapter issue. 

It's ok to compare our Timo Boll VR Table Tennis game to other TT apps. 

But please note that our game is not a physics simulation, as described, it's an arcade-style table tennis game, for the whole family and all levels of experience. And you cannot suppose an apple to taste like an orange, right? 
 
We want to offer action and fun, not only for club players, also for casual players, for everyone! We also aim at making the great world of TT even more popular and more accessible to the public. 

We hope you can relate to this, and it would be great if you could give us another chance and would consider this in your rating. In any case, thanks for your time!

reviews.review_description: For people who want super serious sims, then Eleven might still be your go-to. But after trying a bunch of them, Timo is the best table tennis game for my personal taste. The hit assist feels spot on. The ball goes where you expect without feeling artificial. It's super satisfying and kind of puts you in a flow, Table tennis isn't an inaccessible sport like motorsport or sailing etc. So I don't really need something that simualte the real thing. For a game I want something instantly fun without being frustrating. Would love to see more levels added to keep things fresh!

I had big hopes for great TT game but I’m very disappointed: physics is very bad, serving : not natural, spin- very strange and too big, points in training - I don’t understand it. I’ve tried both ways : with TouchPlus controller and with adapter but in both way it was not comfortable to play it - don’t waste your money - go with Eleven TT

Hi! 

Thanks for your feedback. We're sorry that the experience doesn’t meet your expectations. It's ok to compare our Timo Boll VR Table Tennis game to other TT apps.

reviews.review_description: Waited this game for so long. I have the solidslime adapter, it had a preset for it, but the game seems very off. When serving, 90% of the time the ball goes through the paddle and the rest 10% hits the handle and to serve good is almost impossible. The effect is weirdly powerful and in real life it never goes like this. From a semi-pro perspective is nowhere near the eleven tt game. No matchmaking system, the campaign seems a little fun, but the physics need a lot of improvement.

reviews.review_title: A great game in the making!!
reviews.review_description:
I love this game! 
At first glance, it seems difficult to play, but once you're in, I can tell you it's fantastic! It'll take a lot of updates to really perfect it because there are still a few small flaws, but it's a game with a lot of potential, that's for sure!
You can even recognize the gestures of some professional players.
The best games aren't created in a day!
Thanks to the developers and Timo for the work done so far, and keep it up!

As a new game it’s already much better than many with years of development. Time looks realistic, love to play with full body NPC avatars. Very solid physics and there are tone of content like the campaign mode and training modes. I like the spin visuals and the serve aid as well. Multiplayer is quite barebones at the moment. Can’t believe the eleven fans are already rushing in to kill another quality game. It will be my daily for a couple of weeks and hope to see updates coming.

I pre-ordered the game several months ago, and I was really excited with it. After test it I can say I'm really disappointed with the result. It has nothing to do against the other game, which is 9 years old.
The physics are horrible, you almost don't put force into the stroke and the ball flies off the table. Serves are awful too, I can use the other game to practice my real life serves, with this one I can't replicate even the easy ones. I truly was expecting a high quality game, not this. I was quite worried when I read on your YouTube channel that it was going to be like "Need for Speed", an arcade table tennis game. If you put a VR headset on to play table tennis, you want something as close as real life as possible, then is when you have fun. You missed an opportunity to try to surpass your competitors, specially counting with Timo.

Hi! Thanks for your feedback. We're sorry that the experience doesn’t meet your expectations. It's ok to compare our Timo Boll VR Table Tennis game to other TT apps. reviews.review_description: Compared to Eleven Table Tennis which I play daily, these physics feels very weird. From the tossing of the ball to how it falls on your paddle. When trying to take a shot in the game, the ball goes flying everywhere. Also, the Solidslime Quest 3 preset didn't work for left handed mode, it was way off!.

developer_response.body:
Hi! 

Thanks for your feedback and reporting the SolidSlime issue ; our development team will take care of it asap. 

And we're sorry that the experience doesn’t meet your expectations. It's ok to compare our Timo Boll VR Table Tennis game to other TT apps. 

But please note that our game is not a physics simulation, as described, it's an arcade-style table tennis game, for the whole family and all levels of experience. And you cannot suppose an apple to taste like an orange, right? 
 
We want to offer action and fun, not only for club players, also for casual players, for everyone! We also aim at making the great world of TT even more popular and more accessible to the public. 

We hope you can relate to this, and it would be great if you could give us another chance and would consider this in your rating. In any case, thanks for your time!

reviews.review_title: Great concept, but not there yet.
reviews.review_description:
I like Timo’s avatar and voice, but even though this app isn’t meant to be a full-on simulator, it carries Timo Boll’s name, so the expectation is high. Unfortunately, it’s not there yet.

Even at Basic Level, the physics is just not quite right. The rally pace is way too slow, like playing with a headwind that makes the ball move barely at all, and sometimes it just shoots off. 

Serving doesn’t feel right, and many times, when the AI opponent serves, the ball hits their face before it even reaches the table. And when we're the one serving, the ball is too slow and responds inaccurately. 
The bounce and trajectory are all over the place, and you have to close the racket angle a lot more than you would in real life just to keep shots in. It takes you out of the experience and doesn’t help with real table tennis skills.

The sound effects don’t match either, which makes everything feel off. Also, there’s no way to flip the paddle sides — not just for left- or right-handed players, but also to switch rubber sides. It’d be great if they added that option later, but it’s not a big deal for now.

What would help a lot is being able to adjust things like spin sensitivity, bounce, and arc. That would allow us to, at least, taylor it to match our paddle settings from another TT game.

I can see the potential, but right now it feels more like an early version. I’ll check back in a few months and hope it gets better because training with a TT legend in VR deserves better & realistic experience.

developer_response.body:
Hi! 

Thanks for your feedback. We're sorry that the experience doesn’t meet your expectations at the moment. 

Your feedback has been forwarded to our development team will check the issues you have reported.

But please note that our game is not a physics simulation, as described, it's an arcade-style table tennis game, for the whole family and all levels of experience. And you cannot suppose an apple to taste like an orange, right? 
 
We want to offer action and fun, not only for club players, also for casual players, for everyone! We also aim at making the great world of TT even more popular and more accessible to the public. 

We hope you can relate to this, and it would be great if you could give us another chance and would consider this in your rating. In any case, thanks for your time!

reviews.review_title: Finally a tt game that is fun
reviews.review_description: Been following since the beta.Thank goodness not another TT simulator. We don’t need a hundred of those. I’ve been looking forever for a table tennis GAME that’s actually fun to play, has balanced physics, and look good. Something I can move around with and unwind after work. This hits the spot. Nice work, Timo.

Advertising the ability to train with a legend but to train what exactly? With this weird physics it's hard to say. Apart from that the training is very basic with no proper feedback. The campaign mode is super basic as well.

developer_response.body:
Hi! 

Thanks for your feedback. We're sorry that the experience doesn’t meet your expectations. 

Please note that our game is not a physics simulation, as described, it's an arcade-style table tennis game, for the whole family and all levels of experience. And you cannot suppose an apple to taste like an orange, right? 
 
The training with Timo teaches the TT basics and gives feedback according to what you did in the session.

We want to offer action and fun, not only for club players, also for casual players, for everyone! We also aim at making the great world of TT even more popular and more accessible to the public. 

We hope you can relate to this, and it would be great if you could give us another chance and would consider this in your rating. In any case, thanks for your time!

reviews.review_description:
Maybe I’m the only one who played Timo before Eleven table tennis. But this game feels much more realistic to my in-real-life experience. The throw direction and spin were both quite accurate, whereas I always miss the ball in Eleven. Saying that, my backhand push and forehand loops don’t feel 100% accurate. Especially strong loops, they tend to go too high compared to real world. 

The presentation is top notch. I hope other sports games catch up.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much. Your feedback is greatly appreciated. All the best, Timo Boll Interactive Games.

reviews.review_description: Surprised how enjoyable this is,especially in mixed reality….great tutorial. Money well spent as far as I am concerned. £2.99.  What a bargain

the animation of the opponent (timo) made this experience supper immersive and fun, the physics honestly felt natural to me atleast, and the trainning mode felt pretty useful, and very good bang for your bucks!

developer_response.body: Thank you very much. We appreciate your positive feedback. All the best, Timo Boll Interactive Games

reviews.review_description: Always wanted to get my husband into table tennis and this game does the job. Hitting the ball feels realistic yet forgiving, perfect for what we need.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much. We appreciate your positive feedback. All the best, Timo Boll Interactive Games
